content.full_text:
SUPERSEDED
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
June 14,
1995
Robert K.
Booth, Jr.
City Attorney
City of Los Altos
c/o Atkinson and Farasyn
660 West Dana Street
P.O.
Box 279
Mountain View,
CA
94042
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1-95-169
Dear Mr.
Booth:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of two members of the City of Los Altos Cable Advisory
Committee,
1 pursuant to the conflict—of—interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”) 2
Since your advice request does
not identify the specific governmental official for which you
request this advice,
we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance.
3
1
Pursuant to our telephone conversation of May 19,
1995,
you
indicated that you have been authorized -by the two members to
request this advice.
2
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000
— 18995.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1-95-169
Page2
-
OUESTION
Does the receipt of salary as part-time employees of Foothill
College disqualify two public officials who are members of the
City of Los Altos Cable Television Advisory Committee from
participating in governmental decisions of the committee affecting
the college?
CONCLUSION
The governmental salary from Foothill College is not
considered income which could create a conflict of interest for
the two public officials with respect to decisions of the City of
Los Altos Cable Television Advisory Committee affecting the
college.
However,
a conflict of interest may exist where a
decision will result in their personal expenses,
income, assets,
or liabilities or those of their immediate family increasing or
decreasing by $250.
FACTS
The City of Los Altos has a Cable Television Advisory
Committee, with membership appointed by the city council.
Members
of the committee serve without compensation of any type.
The
committee advises the Los Altos City Council on matters regarding
cable television public access.
The City of Los Altos collects franchise fees from a
privately owned cable franchisee,
and such franchise fees are then
transmitted to Foothill College,
a public community college,
which
operates the Los Altos public access cable channel and provides
programming for it.
One of the purposes of the Cable Television
Advisory Committee is to make recommendations as to the operation
ofthe public access program operated by Fothill Coll-ege under—
contract with the city.
Recommendations to the city may include
how much money and which programs are supportable.
Two members of the city’s Cable Television Advisory Committee
have part-time compensated employment with Foothill College in the
cable television program.-
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making,
participating in making,
or in any way attempting to influence a
governmental decision in which the official knows or has reason to
know he or she has a financial interest.
For purposes of this letter,
we are assuming that members of
the City of Los Altos Cable Television Advisory Committee are


Our File No.
1-95-169
Page3
public officials within the meaning of Section 82048.
The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act prohibit a
public official from participating in a decision that will have a
financial effect on his or her economic interests.
This
prohibition is applied on a decision—by—decision basis.
Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from the effect on the public generally,
on the official or a
member of his or her immediate family or on.:
***
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any position of management.
***
Sections 87103(c)
and (d).
A “public official” is any member of a state or local
government agency.
(Section 82048; Regulation 18700(a).)
Regulation 18700(a) (1)
provides that “member” shall include,
but
not be limited to, salaried or unsalaried members of boards or
commissions with decisionmaking authority.
A board or commission
possesses decisionmaking authority whenever:’
(A)
It may make a
final governmental decision;
(B)
It may compel a governmental
decision; or it may prevent a governmental decision either by
reason of an exclusive power to initiate the decision or by reason
of a veto which may not be overridden; or
(C)
It makes
substantive recommendations which are,
and over an extended period
of time have been, regularly approved without significant amend
ment or modification by another public official or governmental
agency.
If the board meets any of the tests of Regulation
18700(a) (1) (A),
(B)
or
(C), it possesses decisionmaking authority,
and its members are public officials subject to the conflict—of
interest requirements of the Act.
If the board meets none of the
tests, the committee is merely an advisory body and the members
are not subject to the conflict—of—interest requirements of the
Act.


Our File No.
1—95—169
Page
4
The advisory committee members are employed by a governmental
agency, Foothill College.
Therefore, the salary they receive from
the college is not considered income under Section 87103(c)
which
could create a conflict of interest for them with respect to
decisions of the City of Los Altos Cable Television Advisory
Committee affecting the college.
(Curry Advice Letter,
No. A-94-
385.)
Section 8203 0(b) (2),
which defines “income,” exempts salary
and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received from a state,
local,
or federal government agency from the definition of
“income.”
In addition,
a government agency is not an organization or
enterprise operated for profit.
It is, therefore, not a “business
entity” as defined by the Act.
(Section 82005.)
Thus, their
employment with Foothill College will not result in the college
becoming an economic interest of theirs for purposes of Section
87103(d)
However,
Section 87103 states that an official has a
financial interest in a decision if the decision has a material
financial effect on the official or a member of his or her
immediate family.
Thus, the Act provides that a conflict of
interest may exist where a decision will result in a public
official’s personal expenses,
income, assets, or liabilities or
those of his or her immediate family increasing or decreasing by
$250, irrespective of the source of the increase or the decrease.
(Regulation 18702.1(a) (4); Schreiber Advice Letter,
No.
A—88—459;
Torrance Advice Letter,
No.
1-89—142; Fitzpatrick Advice Letter,
No. A-93-308;
Brown Advice Letter,
No. A-93-261; Fisicaro Advice
Letter,
No.
A—94-178.)
However, pursuant to Regulation 18702.1(c),
an official does
not have to disqualify himself or herself from a governmental
- decisioif if the decisiononly affect-s the salary, per diem
1 o
reimbursement for expenses the official or his or her spouse
receives from a state or local government agency.
You state in
your letter that the level of funding for the program or program
changes could result in greater or less employment for the two
committee members.
This necessarily would result in a decision
affecting their governmental salary as exempted in Regulation
18702.1(c).
Therefore, absent some other disqualifying financial
interest as set forth in Section 87103,
or some direct financial
effect on the committee members personally resulting from a
governmental decision,
they will not have conflicts of interest
under the Act due to their part-time employment with Foothill
College.


Our File No.
1-95—169
Page 5.
If you have any questions regarding this matter, please feel
free to contact me at
(916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
IQ__
By:
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division

participating in governmental decisions of the committee affecting
the college?

or liabilities or those of their immediate family increasing or
decreasing by $250.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the receipt of salary as part-time employees of Foothill
College disqualify two public officials who are members of the
City of Los Altos Cable Television Advisory Committee from
participating in governmental decisions of the committee affecting
the college?

CONCLUSION: The governmental salary from Foothill College is not
considered income which could create a conflict of interest for
the two public officials with respect to decisions of the City of
Los Altos Cable Television Advisory Committee affecting the
college.
However,
a conflict of interest may exist where a
decision will result in their personal expenses,
income, assets,
or liabilities or those of their immediate family increasing or
decreasing by $250.
